Party_Details.Name: MCH International Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: MCH International Pte Ltd was a company whose majority shareholder was Mr Wong
MCH International Pte Ltd entered into a Shareholders Agreement with YG Group Pte Ltd and YG Logistics Pte Ltd
MCH International Pte Ltd entered into a Subscription Agreement with YG Group Pte Ltd and YG Logistics Pte Ltd
MCH International Pte Ltd entered into a Loan Agreement with YG Logistics Pte Ltd
MCH International Pte Ltd amended the Loan Agreement with YG Logistics Pte Ltd

Party_Details.Issue: Whether the defendants engaged in a tortious conspiracy against the plaintiffs and whether the company should be wound up on just and equitable grounds
Party_Details.Rule: A claim for conspiracy requires proof of an agreement to do certain acts with the predominant intention to cause injury, or using unlawful means to cause damage
Party_Details.Application: The defendants engaged in a tortious conspiracy and actions that disrupted business operations, causing harm and justifying a winding up of the company on just and equitable grounds.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court dismissed the suit, finding no evidence of conspiracy and concluding that the loss of trust was self-induced by Mr Wong's own breaches of fiduciary duties, making a winding up order inappropriate.

Party_Details.Name: YG Group Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: YG Group Pte Ltd was incorporated in Singapore
YG Group Pte Ltd entered into a Shareholders Agreement with MCH International Pte Ltd and YG Logistics Pte Ltd
YG Group Pte Ltd entered into a Subscription Agreement with MCH International Pte Ltd and YG Logistics Pte Ltd
YG Group Pte Ltd executed an amendment agreement in respect of the Subscription Agreement on 2015-08-18
YG Group Pte Ltd executed a Sale and Purchase Agreement with the Vendors

Party_Details.Issue: Whether the defendants engaged in a tortious conspiracy against the plaintiffs and whether the company should be wound up on just and equitable grounds
Party_Details.Rule: A claim for conspiracy requires proof of an agreement to do certain acts with the predominant intention to cause injury, or using unlawful means to cause damage
Party_Details.Application: There was no conspiracy, and the actions taken by the defendants were reasonable responses within the scope of the agreements, so winding up the company is unwarranted.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court dismissed the suit, finding no evidence of conspiracy and concluding that the loss of trust was self-induced by Mr Wong's own breaches of fiduciary duties, making a winding up order inappropriate.

Party_Details.Name: MCH International Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: MCH International Pte Ltd entered into a Loan Agreement with YG Logistics Pte Ltd
MCH International Pte Ltd amended the Loan Agreement with YG Logistics Pte Ltd
MCH International Pte Ltd failed to recognize the appointment of Mr. Tan as proxy

Party_Details.Issue: Whether the defendants by counterclaim engaged in a lawful or unlawful means conspiracy by usurping board control and commencing legal actions
Party_Details.Rule: A party may claim damages for conspiracy if it can prove an agreement to cause injury via unlawful acts or lawful acts done with the predominant intention to harm
Party_Details.Application: The defendants by counterclaim engaged in a lawful or unlawful means conspiracy by usurping board control and filing suits with the predominant intention of causing financial harm.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court dismissed the counterclaim, finding that the actions of the defendants by counterclaim were lawful and pursued to further their legitimate self-interests rather than with a predominant intention to injure the plaintiffs.

Party_Details.Name: YG Logistics Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: YG Logistics Pte Ltd entered into a Loan Agreement with MCH International Pte Ltd
YG Logistics Pte Ltd amended the Loan Agreement with MCH International Pte Ltd
YG Logistics Pte Ltd exercised its rights under the Deed of Share Charge

Party_Details.Issue: Whether the defendants by counterclaim engaged in a lawful or unlawful means conspiracy by usurping board control and commencing legal actions
Party_Details.Rule: A party may claim damages for conspiracy if it can prove an agreement to cause injury via unlawful acts or lawful acts done with the predominant intention to harm
Party_Details.Application: The actions taken were completely lawful and pursued to further legitimate commercial self-interests, with no predominant intention to injure the plaintiffs by counterclaim.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court dismissed the counterclaim, finding that the actions of the defendants by counterclaim were lawful and pursued to further their legitimate self-interests rather than with a predominant intention to injure the plaintiffs.
